records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: Every accepted material effect in the finite boundary transition model requires a live same-epoch grant within the caller ceiling and a prior dispatch receipt; the source-anchored nominal path reaches independent observation and exact local rollback.

records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model

records.chapter_title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Missing backup state blocks full-state binding.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Missing descendant state blocks full-state binding.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Missing prospective checkpoint authority or selection rule blocks full-state binding.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: A declared-surface rollback mismatch blocks the update record and cannot be generalized to semantic or production recovery.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Behavioral cohort change cannot serve as evidence of causal influence reduction in deletion-claim adjudication.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Behavioral cohort change cannot serve as evidence of privacy protection or erasure in deletion-claim adjudication.

records.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
records.formal_target: Lineage propagation or invalidation cannot serve as evidence of physical storage or backup erasure in deletion-claim adjudication.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward-managed work contract records objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims before dispatch.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward action that exceeds treasury policy, changes governance rules, or touches protected assets cannot execute without explicit approval evidence.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A stewarded release publication requires test, evidence, changelog, residual, and approval records.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: When sunset criteria are met, ordinary work generation is blocked until a sunset review is opened.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward lifecycle route sends tainted unreviewed events to quarantine, sunset criteria without review to sunset review, autonomy escalation without charter approval to approval, and over-policy treasury spend to approval.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward contribution ledger keeps authorship, review, evidence, compensation, reputation, governance effect, and conflicts separated; collapsed governance scoring is rejected and support-state changes require evidence-transition records.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward federation contract requires scoped work contracts, bounded worker authority, tool/data/budget gates, external-spend approval, and evidence-bundle requirements before dispatch.

records.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
records.formal_target: Every accepted finite cross-layer trace joins exact parent artifacts and canonical state across typed layers, preserves the authority ceiling, and cannot widen active authority.

records.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
records.formal_target: A cross-layer transition with a parent/state fork, missing governance gate, residual erasure, effect after revocation, unacknowledged terminal effect, missing receipt, or incomplete rollback is rejected or contained in quarantine.

records.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
records.formal_target: The source-anchored finite consumer composes approved, blocked, rolled-back, and quarantined traces while preserving joined artifact/state lineage, authority, effect acknowledgement, evidence, residual, terminal-receipt, and no-promotion boundaries and rejecting targeted mutations.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: An implementation-reference claim names the report, config, or tool surface and does not rely on dashboard prose alone.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked when required gate reports are absent or failing.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A public-safe Theseus report-bundle audit fixture is accepted only when bundle components, replay-readiness rows, crosswalk rows, gate mappings, work-board contract fields, visible artifact gaps, intervention-ladder ordering, and no-promotion boundaries are complete.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A public-safe Theseus public task-bundle import fixture is accepted only when source reports, metadata-only public task boundaries, zero public training rows, zero exported prompts/tests/solutions/traces/scores/candidate code, operator and benchmark gates, visible artifact gaps, no clean-live-replay claim, and no-promotion boundaries are complete.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: The selected Theseus/Fast support-lane aggregate carries 4 command replays, 16 tracked artifacts, 68 public task records, 14 expected-invalid or rejected controls, 2 accepted no-promotion decisions, the four included support surfaces, and explicit no-clean-live-replay, no-model-quality, no-generation-speed, no-useful-solution-model, no-chapter-core-promotion, and no-support-state-promotion boundaries.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus artifact-retention replay import fixture records exact-hash retained-artifact replay, public-safe redaction, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, required record counts, and rejects hash mismatch or chapter-core promotion overclaim.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus module definition-of-done import fixture records a GREEN gate, 22 ready module records, 0 hard gaps, 0 warnings, source-backlog routing, public-safety boundaries, and rejects chapter-core promotion or capability overclaim.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus project-registry import fixture records 5,662 registered paths, 24 owned lifecycle surfaces, complete registry coverage, zero unregistered active sources, zero stale or missing report outputs, zero generated source artifacts, zero registry-governance violations, public-safety boundaries, and rejects unregistered-source, clean-live-replay, private-payload, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus assistant reference-trace import fixture records 19 required trace record types, 27/27 gates, 2,203 route-validator view records, 12 selected VCM pages, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, public-safety boundaries, and rejects missing-hop, private-payload, clean-live-replay, model-quality, benchmark-headline, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus accelerator parity manifest import fixture records 7 surfaces, 7 MLX report summaries, 4 Metal report summaries, 4 artifact manifests, 4 scheduler-canary surfaces, zero hard failures, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, no teacher use, locked model promotion and production routing, public-safety boundaries, and rejects full-parity, production-routing, model-promotion, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus book-to-Theseus crosswalk import fixture records trigger state `GREEN`, 53 public-safe pointer rows, 20 backlog cards, 134 source-sync review decisions, public-safety boundaries, and rejects source-sync failure, public-safety failure, clean-live-replay overclaim, and chapter-core-promotion overclaim.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A sanitized Project Theseus work-board metadata import fixture records 130 durable task rows, 412 event rows, 133 evidence rows, five SQLite tables, stale-snapshot currentness blocking, public-safety boundaries, and rejects clean-live-replay, private-payload, public-training-row, and chapter-core-promotion overclaims.

records.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap
records.formal_target: A roadmap phase can unlock a dependent phase only after acceptance gates pass.

records.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap
records.formal_target: A phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.

records.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap
records.formal_target: A modeled prototype phase-gate fixture bridge accepts only the complete public-safe fixture summary and rejects missing non-claim boundaries while preserving no support-state promotion and no phase-completion claim.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: Every chapter in the manifest has outline proof targets and generated claim placeholders.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: A structural update without regenerated scaffold/proof manifest is invalid.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: A public-surface change packet requires validation, changelog, support-state, non-claim, derivative-boundary, and evidence-transition discipline.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: A current reader release-candidate route cannot treat local format evidence as approval while accessibility, audio, release-approval, or non-claim blockers remain.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id.
